generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Controls vehicle speed via adaptive cruise algorithms based on lead vehicle distance and traffic conditions"
]

instance EmergencyBrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Executes emergency braking commands triggered remotely via Digital Twin"
]

instance VehicleSteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Manages steering inputs for path following and obstacle avoidance"
]

instance SpeedSettingActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Sets desired vehicle speed commands based on adaptive cruise logic"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Point cloud sensor capturing 2D spatial data for lead vehicle and environmental object detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time vehicle state data including motor, steering, and overall vehicle parameters"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateMetrics
]

instance MotorActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors motor performance and speed for adaptive cruise control feedback"
]

instance SteeringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks steering angle and vehicle orientation for path planning"
]

instance ServoActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Records servo position data for precise vehicle control"
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance PointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor, MotorActuatorSensor, SteeringSensor, ServoActuatorSensor
]

instance ClusteringTrackingResults : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPath : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateMetrics
]

// C5: Data Components
instance RawLiDARPoints : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed point cloud data from LiDAR sensors (2D points)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state metrics including speed, distance, and operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateMetrics
]

instance ObjectDetectionTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed clustering and tracking results identifying lead vehicles and environmental objects"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ClusteringTrackingResults
]

instance DrivingPathProjection : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived projected driving path based on sensor data and control algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedDrivingPath
]

instance SpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Adaptive cruise speed commands calculated by Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateParameters
]

// C10: Models
instance AdaptiveCruiseModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Distance-based adaptive cruise control algorithm model implementing lead vehicle distance regulation"
]

instance LiDARProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Point cloud processing model for clustering and tracking of environmental objects"
]

instance HighwayEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Highway-specific driving environment model for ACC applications"
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DistanceBasedACCEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based computation offloading framework for distance-based adaptive cruise control algorithms"
]

instance LiDARDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component handling LiDAR point cloud preprocessing and clustering"
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 framework enabling internal communication between physical sensors and Digital Twin components"
]

instance UDPTransportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UDP transport layer for low-latency inter-entity communication between physical twin and Digital Twin"
]

instance OPCUAProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OPC Unified Architecture protocol for interfacing with physical sensors and actuators"
]

instance CloudComputeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud infrastructure hosting compute-intensive Digital Twin tasks for ACC offloading"
]

instance LowLatencyMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware ensuring real-time synchronization between physical and digital components"
]

instance CustomVisualizationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-built user interface framework for real-time visualization of vehicle state and environment"
]

instance ReportingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data aggregation and reporting system for generating vehicle performance reports"
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical tracking of ACC-relevant vehicle state data including speed, distance to lead vehicles, and projected driving path"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, HistoricalPerformanceInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delegates ACC-specific computations (lead vehicle detection, speed adjustment) from vehicle ECUs to cloud-based Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpeedAdjustmentAction, LeadVehicleDetectionAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables geographically remote control and operation of ACC system with safety-critical constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakeCommand, SpeedSettingCommand
]

instance DataPreprocessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Handles data transformation from raw sensor inputs to structured formats for Digital Twin processing"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAlignmentAction, UnitConversionAction
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements adaptive cruise control functionality with dynamic speed regulation based on lead vehicle distance"
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live visualization of vehicle speed, distance to lead vehicles, and projected driving path"
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Post-event analysis of vehicle performance metrics and operational logs"
]

instance SpeedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Computes and executes optimal speed commands based on distance to lead vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Processes LiDAR data to identify and track lead vehicles in the driving environment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakeCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immediately triggers emergency braking system based on remote operator input"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SpeedSettingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sets desired vehicle speed via Digital Twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataAlignmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensures consistency between different data streams and units across the system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UnitConversionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Converts raw sensor data into standardized units for Digital Twin processing"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DistanceBasedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts vehicle speed dynamically based on calculated distance to lead vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance HighwayDrivingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator, EmergencyBrakeActuator,
                 VehicleStateSensor, LiDARSensor, MotorActuatorSensor,
                 SteeringSensor, ServoActuatorSensor,
                 AdaptiveCruiseModel, HighwayEnvironmentModel,
                 MonitoringService, RemoteManagementService,
                 ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARDataProcessingEnabler, ROS2CommunicationEnabler,
             UDPTransportEnabler, CloudComputeEnabler,
             CustomVisualizationFramework, ReportingFramework,
             DataPreprocessingService, AdaptiveCruiseControlService
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components, with a **100 ms accuracy requirement** for computation offloading and real-time coordination. This is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where deviations beyond this threshold could disrupt critical operations. The system relies on hardware-imposed constraints that mandate precise temporal alignment, ensuring deterministic execution of ROS2 nodes across distributed entities (e.g., User Entity, Digital Twin Entity, Physical Twin Entity). The **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)** does not explicitly define synchronization protocols, but the 100 ms latency threshold is explicitly tied to hardware limitations and safety-critical timing demands."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)** for architectural design, organizing components into three primary entities:
    - **User Entity**: Hosts the singular *User Interface FE*, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components.
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data pre-processing, actuation, and control mechanisms (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking).
    - **Physical Twin Entity**: Reflects the twinned system’s inherent resources (compute, sensors, actuators) and general automotive functionality, deviating from ISO 23247’s OME by consolidating device-specific and cross-cutting logic.

    **Component Integration**:
    - Each entity is implemented as a **ROS2 package**, with nodes executing within FE-specific scopes (e.g., a *Data Storage* FE not directly mapped to a single ISO 23247 FE).
    - **Resource Management**: Hardware deployment aligns entities to physical components (e.g., sensors/actuators in Physical Twin Entity), enabling distributed execution but introducing **ownership trade-offs** (e.g., general automotive logic in Physical Twin Entity vs. ISO 23247’s OME specialization).
    - **System-Wide Coordination**: The system lacks explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., no mention of middleware like Zephyr or Kafka for inter-entity messaging). Instead, **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model** underpins data exchange between FE nodes, with temporal constraints enforced via hardware synchronization (100 ms accuracy)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard outlines a **reference architecture (RA) for Digital Twins in manufacturing**, but does not explicitly define lifecycle stages. However, the standard implicitly implies a structured lifecycle based on its **entity-based RA**, where lifecycle governance is managed through **modular, entity-driven development**:

1. **Development Phase**:
   - **Initialization**: Creation of **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**—physical assets like equipment, facilities, or processes—with corresponding **Digital Twin Entities (DTEs)**.
   - **Data Collection & Control Setup**: Implementation of **Data Collection and Device Control Entities** using protocols like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for real-time data exchange and control interfacing.

2. **Representation Stages**:
   - **Conceptual Phase**: Abstract modeling of OMEs as **Functional Entities (FEs)**—e.g., data transformation units or user interfaces—without physical integration.
   - **Operational Phase**: Real-time synchronization between DTEs and OMEs via **entity-level data streams**, ensuring **observable consistency** (e.g., monitoring equipment status, process parameters).

3. **Transition Management**:
   - The standard does not specify explicit transition protocols, but lifecycle governance relies on **version-controlled FE implementations** (e.g., OPC UA’s versioning for data models) and **modular updates** to DTEs without disrupting OME operations.
   - **Lifecycle governance** is implicitly handled through **entity collaboration** (e.g., DTEs communicating with OMEs via standardized interfaces).

**Note**: No explicit lifecycle stages (e.g., 'Design → Deployment → Maintenance') or governance frameworks (e.g., ISO 15288) are detailed in the provided documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin’s **fidelity** is governed by the **ISO 23247** reference architecture, where the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)** must accurately mirror the **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)** through synchronized data flows. Validation and verification are implicitly supported by the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, which ensures real-time consistency between physical and digital representations. Uncertainty quantification and error detection are not explicitly detailed, but the framework implies iterative model refinement via feedback loops between OMEs and the DTE. Quality assurance processes are implied through adherence to ISO standards and the use of standardized protocols (e.g., OPC UA) for data exchange, ensuring robustness in model accuracy and data integrity.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - Internal SW communication: ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for software components.
    - Inter-entity communication: Wi-Fi over UDP (User Datagram Protocol) for transport layer communication.

  - **Network Topology & Architecture:**
    - The architecture diagram (Fig. 2) shows a modular deployment where sensor data flows through a series of FE components (e.g., Data Pre-Processing, Actuation FE) before reaching the digital twin visualization.
    - Data Translation component acts as a central translator for all data streams, ensuring compatibility across message types and units.

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - Real-time processing is critical for vehicle state data (e.g., motor/steering actuation), implying low-latency requirements (likely <100ms for critical paths).
    - Bandwidth requirements are not explicitly quantified but are necessary for high-frequency LiDAR data (e.g., 10-100Hz) and real-time visualization.

  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly detailed in the documents, but ROS2 supports fault tolerance and redundancy for critical components.
    - Wi-Fi UDP may lack redundancy; potential for error handling is implied but not specified.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - No explicit mention of security protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) for network communication. Data Translation component ensures unit alignment but does not imply security.
    "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Deployment:** Likely, given the real-time processing requirements for vehicle state data (e.g., motor/steering actuation) and sensor inputs (LiDAR).
    - **On-Premise:** Possible for localized vehicle applications (e.g., autonomous vehicles in controlled environments).
    - **Cloud:** Not explicitly mentioned; however, data storage (e.g., report generation) could theoretically be offloaded to cloud services if needed.

  - **Computational and Storage Requirements:**
    - **Computational:**
      - Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE requires significant processing power for high-resolution LiDAR data.
      - Actuation FE and Data Pre-Processing FE need real-time computational capabilities for vehicle control.
    - **Storage:**
      - Data Storage component handles raw and processed data (e.g., reports, visualizations).
      - Scalability for storage is implied but not quantified (e.g., for long-term vehicle telemetry).

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - ROS2 supports distributed systems, allowing horizontal scaling of FE components (e.g., adding more nodes for clustering/tracking).
    - Wi-Fi UDP network can scale with additional entities (e.g., more vehicles) but may face congestion if not managed.

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-Device:** For embedded systems (e.g., vehicle onboard units).
    - **Edge Computing:** For localized processing (e.g., real-time visualization).
    - **Hybrid:** Combination of edge and cloud for storage/reporting.

  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - No explicit SLAs or redundancy guarantees are mentioned, but ROS2 and Wi-Fi UDP are designed for reliability in distributed systems.
    - Critical paths (e.g., motor/steering actuation) may rely on redundant sensor data or fallback mechanisms (not detailed).
    "
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must adhere to **data governance frameworks** ensuring explicit **data ownership policies** where physical and digital entities (e.g., vehicles, infrastructure) are distinctly attributed to stakeholders (e.g., manufacturers, operators, or regulatory bodies). Privacy protection is enforced through compliance with **data protection regulations** such as those implied by ISO 26262-1 (2018), which mandates strict adherence to **functional safety standards** for autonomous systems, indirectly requiring safeguards against unauthorized data exposure.

For **consent management**, the system must incorporate **explicit user or entity consent mechanisms** for data collection and processing, particularly in **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as defined in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 (2018)**, where **service requirements** for V2X communications necessitate **secure consent protocols** to ensure compliance with privacy laws. **Data anonymization** is implied through **ISO 26262-1’s** emphasis on **safety-critical data integrity**, where anonymization techniques (e.g., differential privacy, pseudonymization) must be applied to prevent **safety-critical data breaches** in autonomous systems.

Data sharing agreements are governed by **technical specifications** in **3GPP TS 22.185**, where **V2X service providers** must establish **non-disclosure agreements (NDAs)** and **data access controls** to prevent unauthorized sharing of real-time sensor data (e.g., vehicle telemetry, traffic conditions). The **model-driven architecture (MDA)** proposed in Dalibor et al. (2020) for **interactive digital twin cockpits** suggests that **privacy-preserving ontologies** (e.g., using **OWL/DL reasoning**) could be employed to enforce **fine-grained access control** over Twin data, ensuring compliance with **GDPR-like frameworks** in high-stakes domains like autonomous driving."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard is the **core compliance framework** for Digital Twins in manufacturing, with the following specifications:

1. **Industry Standards & Specifications**:
   - **ISO 23247-2 (Reference Architecture)**: Defines an **entity-based RA** with four core entities (Data Collection, Digital Twin, User, OME) and **Functional Entities (FEs)**.
   - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: Mandated for **data exchange** between entities (e.g., Data Collection Entity ↔ Digital Twin Entity).
   - **No other explicit standards** are referenced in the documents.

2. **Certification & Compliance**:
   - The standard does not specify **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks**, but adherence to **ISO 23247-2** ensures interoperability via OPC UA.

3. **Interoperability Standards**:
   - **OPC UA** is the **primary interoperability protocol**, enabling cross-entity communication (e.g., DTE ↔ OME).
   - **No additional interoperability frameworks** (e.g., IEC 61499) are mentioned.

**Public Demonstration**: A publicly available demonstration of the DT is referenced ([link](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin)), though its compliance specifics are not detailed in the documents.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must integrate **cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **ISO 15622 (2018)** for **adaptive cruise control (ACC)** systems, where **performance requirements** include **real-time threat detection** (e.g., intrusion detection systems (IDS) for V2X communications). **Access control mechanisms** are enforced via **role-based access control (RBAC)** and **attribute-based access control (ABAC)**, particularly in **ISO 26262-1 (2018)**, which mandates **safety-critical authentication** (e.g., **ECDSA or elliptic curve cryptography**) for **functional safety-critical operations**.

**Authentication systems** are standardized in **3GPP TS 22.185 (2018)**, where **V2X services** require **mutual authentication** between vehicles and infrastructure using **X.509 certificates** or **short-lived tokens** to prevent spoofing attacks. **Threat mitigation strategies** include **denial-of-service (DoS) protection** for V2X networks, leveraging **rate-limiting** and **packet filtering** as per **3GPP’s security architecture**. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are embedded in **ISO 26262-1**, where **safety-related Twin components** must fail in a **predictable, non-hazardous state** upon cyber-physical failure (e.g., **hardware watchdogs**, **firmware rollback procedures**).

**Risk assessment approaches** are implicitly tied to **ISO 26262-1’s** **safety lifecycle**, where **hazard analysis (HAZOP)** and **failure mode analysis (FMEA)** are applied to Twin data streams. The **model-driven architecture (MDA)** in Dalibor et al. (2020) suggests **automated risk quantification** using **probabilistic safety models** (e.g., **Markov chains**) to validate Twin safety under cyber-physical attacks. **Safety-critical system considerations** extend to **real-time synchronization** of Twin models with physical systems, ensuring **latency budgets** (e.g., <10ms for ACC) are met via **deterministic communication protocols** (e.g., **Ethernet-PTP, CAN FD**)."
]
